task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the requirements, but handling the edge case of an empty list could be more explicitly mentioned.
trace: In this solution, I first handle the edge case of an empty list by returning None. Then, for a non-empty list, I sort the numbers and remove one occurrence of both the minimum and maximum values, while also adjusting for the odd-numbered length cases. Finally, I calculate the sum of the trimmed numbers and return it as the trimmed mean after dividing by the number of remaining elements minus 2. This function ensures that only one occurrence of the minimum and maximum is removed.
